title: Delhi government provides heatwave relief to 265,000 people through mobile units and cooling zones - The News Mill
full_text:
Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has launched extensive relief measures to support residents amid severe heatwave conditions in the national capital. Special provisions through Mobile Heat Relief Units and Cooling Zones have been implemented across all 13 districts to protect citizens, labourers, commuters, and vulnerable groups from extreme heat, according to the chief minister’s office.
The Delhi government aims not only to administer the city but also to ensure the safety and comfort of every individual. “During this period of extreme heat, the government is working on the ground with full sensitivity so that no person suffers because of the heat,” the Chief Minister stated.
These initiatives, including mobile heat relief units and cooling zones, demonstrate the government’s commitment to public service and a humanitarian approach. The administration continues to monitor the situation closely and plans to enhance relief efforts as needed.
The Revenue Department oversees the entire operation. Between May 6 and May 23, 2026, approximately 265,000 people across all districts received aid through the Mobile Heat Relief Units. During this timeframe, about 113,900 litres of cold drinking water were distributed to prevent heat-related dehydration; over 100,000 Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) packets were also provided to protect against heatstroke.
Additional items distributed included 44,646 gamchas and 30,864 caps to shield individuals from direct sunlight. The campaign particularly reached large numbers in the North-East, East, South-West, and New Delhi districts. The Chief Minister emphasized that the mobile units’ locations are rotated daily to maximise relief coverage in crowded areas and among those in need. This campaign will continue until July.
In addition, 14 Cooling Zones have been established in various districts, offering a place to rest and respite from the heat. These zones cater specifically to citizens, rickshaw pullers, labourers, and outdoor workers. Each Cooling Zone is equipped with seating for 100 people, cold drinking water, five desert coolers, and five high-speed fans to maintain comfort. ORS packets, caps, gamchas, and first-aid kits are also available. Ten Civil Defence volunteers assist in each location’s operation.
Currently, five Cooling Zones function in the Old Delhi district, with one each in the North-East, North, Central North, and Central districts, and five in the East district. Similar facilities are set to open in the remaining districts within the week.
Chief Minister Gupta remarked, “In a metropolitan city like Delhi, where even a glass of cold water is often not easily available to people, these initiatives launched by the government are providing real relief.” She added that public response to these arrangements has been very positive and affirmed the government’s full commitment to safeguard residents from the heatwave.

title: Delhi traffic police use tech helmets to protect officers in 43°C heatwave - The News Mill
full_text:
New Delhi is experiencing a severe heatwave with temperatures reaching 43°C. In response, the Delhi Traffic Police have introduced innovative equipment, including air-conditioned helmets and portable fans, to help officers endure extreme conditions while managing heavy traffic.
The cooling helmets are currently being trialled among personnel who spend extended periods in direct sunlight. Assistant Sub-Inspector Veer Singh described the technology as transformative, stating, “This helmet keeps us relaxed in this weather. It can work easily for 4-5 hours, depending on the charge in its battery…”
The battery-powered cooling system provides effective relief for 4 to 5 hours, enabling officers to maintain comfort during peak heat periods. Additionally, mobile vans have been deployed to support officers managing VIP movements, serving as hydration and nutrition hubs to prevent exhaustion.
The initiative coincides with Red and Orange heat alerts issued by the India Meteorological Department across several states, indicating dangerously high temperatures.
Dr Randeep Guleria, Chairman of the Institute of Internal Medicine, Respiratory & Sleep Medicine at Medanta, warned of serious health risks associated with prolonged heat exposure. While minor issues such as prickly heat can be managed, he cautioned, “Prolonged exposure to extreme heat can trigger heat stroke, a potentially life-threatening condition in which the body loses its ability to regulate temperature.”
As the heatwave persists across North India, the Delhi Traffic Police’s approach highlights critical measures to protect frontline workers operating in hazardous conditions.

title: दिल्ली-एनसीआर में जून के पहले हफ्ते गर्मी से राहत, IMD ने जारी किया आंधी-बारिश का अलर्ट - ETV Bharat
search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Delhi
full_text:
दिल्ली-एनसीआर में जून के पहले हफ्ते गर्मी से राहत, IMD ने जारी किया आंधी-बारिश का अलर्ट
IMD के मुताबिक 1 जून से 5 जून तक बारिश का मौसम बना रह सकता है.
नई दिल्ली: दिल्ली-एनसीआर के लिए मौसम विभाग ने अच्छी खबर दी है. जून के महीने में जहां झुलसाने वाली गर्मी लोगों को परेशान करती है, लेकिन इस बार जून का पहला हफ्ता बारिश और बूंदाबांदी वाला रहने वाला है.
मौसम विभाग ने राहत भरी खबर दी है, जिसके मुताबिक लोगों को जून के शुरू में झुलसाने वाली गर्मी से राहत मिलने के संकेत है. IMD के मुताबिक 1 जून से 5 जून तक बारिश का मौसम बना रह सकता है. हालांकि उसके बाद गर्मी अपना असर दिखाना शुरू कर सकती है.
कैसा रहा जून का पहला दिन
दिल्ली में कल जून के पहले दिन सोमवार को दिल्ली का अधिकतम तापमान 36.3 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया, जो सामान्य से 3.7 डिग्री कम है. वहीं, न्यूनतम तापमान सामान्य से 2.0 डिग्री नीचे 24.9 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा. हवा में नमी का स्तर 84 से 42 प्रतिशत रहा.
तापमान में आई गिरावट
मौसम विभाग का अनुमान है कि मंगलवार को दिल्ली का न्यूनतम तापमान 24 डिग्री जबकि अधिकतम तापमान 37 डिग्री सेल्सियस के आसपास रह सकता है. तापमान 36-38 डिग्री सेल्सियस के बीच रहने की संभावना है.
आसमान में छाए बादल
IMD के अनुसार, आज आंशिक रुप से आसमान में बादल छाये रह सकते हैं और कभी-कभी आसमान पूरी तरह बादलों से घिर सकता है. इस दौरान हल्की बारिश के साथ गरज-चमक का पूर्वानुमान लगाया है. इस दौरान हवा की गति 40-50 किमी/घंटा तक पहुंच सकती है.
IMD का अनुमान है कि अगले 6 दिनों के दौरान अधिकतम तापमान में 2-3 डिग्री सेल्सियस की वृद्धि हो सकती है. वहीं, न्यूनतम तापमान में अगले 24 घंटों के दौरान कोई बड़ा बदलाव नहीं होगा.
AQI में हुआ सुधार
केंद्रीय प्रदूषण एवं नियंत्रण बोर्ड (CPCB) के अनुसार राजधानी दिल्ली में शुक्रवार सुबह 7:15 बजे तक औसतन वायु गुणवत्ता सूचकांक 97 अंक बना हुआ है, जबकि दिल्ली एनसीआर के शहर फरीदाबाद में 88, गुरुग्राम में 93, गाजियाबाद में 95, ग्रेटर नोएडा में 82 और नोएडा में 78 अंक बना हुआ है। राजधानी दिल्ली के अधिकांश इलाकों में AQI लेवल 100 से नीचे बना हुआ है। जबकि कुछ इलाकों में AQI लेवल 100 से ऊपर और 200 के बीच में बना हुआ है.
